content.claim: Hungary tracks students earlier and more intensively than OECD norms: approximately 85% of Hungarian students are in schools with academic selection capacity, compared to OECD average of 43%, with tracking beginning at age 10-13 versus OECD average of age 15.
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Snapshot comparison at single time point
Does not capture quality or enforcement variation of selection mechanisms across schools
Selection capacity does not necessarily equal equal intensity of use
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
